elements.text: limitations and statute of repose. The tolling agreement shall be executed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and Freddie Mac within seven days of initiation of the IDR Process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Once either party initiates the IDR Process, both the Servicer and Freddie Mac shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: follow the process set forth in the IDR Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. No new information, facts or documents, or Servicing Corrections pursuant to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3602.2(b), may be submitted by the Servicer, and no new information, facts or
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents may be submitted by Freddie Mac as part of the IDR Process
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Other considerations
elements.label: text

elements.text: At any time during the Appeal Process or any processes set forth herein, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: propose a Servicing Repurchase Alternative. Freddie Mac will, in good faith, review and
elements.label: text

elements.text: advise the Servicer of its decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may offer or decline to offer the Servicer certain Servicing Repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatives based on the Servicer Counterparty Status to the extent there are future
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations required as part of the Servicing Repurchase Alternative. Other factors to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered by Freddie Mac may include, but are not limited to, the failure to maintain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality Servicing practice and the Servicer’s ability and willingness to comply with other
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of the Guide and the other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer does not comply with the terms of the Servicing Remedy letter and does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not remit payment or initiate the Appeal Process or any processes set forth herein timely,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may then pursue other rights and remedies for failure to comply, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase; however, the repurchase appeal provisions of this Section 3602.4(c) will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be available to the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 3603: Termination of Servicing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 3603.1: Termination of Servicing Contracts and related
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicing Contract Rights (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Termination of Servicing Contracts and related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights related to sale of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Termination of Servicing Contracts and related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may terminate either a Servicer’s entire Servicing Contract or any portion of it,
elements.label: text

elements.text: along with related Servicing Contract Rights. This termination can occur, in whole or in part,
elements.label: text

elements.text: at any time, with cause or without cause as specified in this chapter. See Section 3603.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: below for requirements for termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights in connection with the sale of Mortgages by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A termination of a given portion of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights shall not waive or impair Freddie Mac’s future right, in the exercise of its sole
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion, to terminate all or further portions of the remaining Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Servicing Contract for any reason listed in Section 2301.1(b), regardless of whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: the reason relates to Mortgages subject to the termination of the given portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, Freddie Mac may use its sole discretion in selecting which Mortgages shall be
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to a partial termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of termination of all or a portion of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights, Freddie Mac may also require the Servicer to engage a Servicing Agent for
elements.label: text

elements.text: all or a portion of the Mortgages, for such period of time as determined by Freddie Mac. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Agent must be approved by Freddie Mac, in its sole discretion, and the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with all other applicable requirements of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete the retention and use of the Servicing Agent by the Servicer within such time
elements.label: text

elements.text: as specified by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be responsible for all expenses and costs incurred with respect to the Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whether the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights are terminated or
elements.label: text

elements.text: involve the engagement of a Servicing Agent, the Servicer remains fully liable to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac for all Servicing representations and warranties as detailed in Section 8102.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s election of a remedy under this section does not waive its right to elect any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other remedy or combination of remedies, including termination of all or a portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights. The Transferee Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption of responsibilities, representations and warranties upon a Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not release the Transferor Servicer of its responsibilities, representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: with respect to the transferred Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Termination with cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may terminate all or any portion of the Servicing Contract and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract Rights by the Servicer at any time with cause for any of the reasons
elements.label: text

elements.text: cited in Section 2301.1(b) regarding disqualification and/or suspension.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 2301.1(c) through 2301.3 govern the notice of termination of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights with cause, along with the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: opportunity to appeal, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights with cause is
elements.label: text

elements.text: a basis for immediate disqualification or suspension as a Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Transfer of Servicing with cause does not relieve the Servicer of any of its obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Purchase Documents with respect to the Mortgages related to such Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Servicer compensation/Freddie Mac reimbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: with cause, the Servicer will have no right to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Service the applicable Mortgages for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receive any compensation for performing such Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be paid a Termination Fee, as detailed in Section 3603.1(a)(ii)(B) below
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights are terminated with
elements.label: text

elements.text: cause and Freddie Mac appoints a Transferee Servicer, Freddie Mac is entitled to
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement from the former Servicer for any loss, damage or expense incident to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transfer of Servicing. Such expenses include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Court costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reasonable attorney fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copying costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Costs of the physical transfer of files
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cost of an audit or examination of the Servicer’s records, if Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that such a procedure is appropriate (“transfer-related expenses”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The transfer requirements in Section 3603.2(a), the documents and records
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 3603.3(a), and the remittance to Freddie Mac requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3603.3(b) apply to any termination of the Servicing Contract and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract Rights with cause.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Termination without cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Notice period and Servicer right to transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac maintains the right to terminate all or any portion of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights at any time without cause with respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to any Mortgage owned or guaranteed by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In such cases:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Freddie Mac will provide written notice to the Servicer 90 days prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled date of the termination date
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. During this 90-day period (the “Notice Period”), the Servicer may initiate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing to a Transferee Servicer approved by Freddie Mac for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific Servicing portfolio being transferred. This Transfer of Servicing must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the requirements of Chapter 7101. If approved, the transfer must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: finalized within 60 days following Freddie Mac’s approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is unable to facilitate a Transfer of Servicing within the Notice Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: or fails to complete an approved Transfer of Servicing on time, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights and proceed
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a Transfer of Servicing to a Transferee Servicer chosen by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Termination fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac terminates the Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights without cause and engages in a Transfer of Servicing to a Transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer of Freddie Mac’s choice (under Section 3603.1(a)(ii)(A) above), Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will pay the terminated Servicer a termination fee (“Termination Fee”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated based on conditions existing as of the transfer date in an amount equal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Two times the Net Servicing Rate, as defined below, multiplied by the UPB of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferring Mortgages that are not delinquent as of the transfer date. (No
elements.label: text

elements.text: Termination Fee will be paid for Mortgages that are delinquent as of the transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: date.) The following apply to delinquent Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For purposes of this calculation, a Mortgage will be deemed to be delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: if any payment is outstanding:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As of a month end of the Effective Date of Transfer, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In the event of a Transfer of Servicing that takes place other than at month
elements.label: text

elements.text: end, as of the month end immediately preceding the Effective Date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ No Termination Fee will be paid for a delinquent Mortgage as of the Effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The market value of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: to service the Mortgages as established by a qualified market leader in servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: valuations using costs reflective of Freddie Mac’s costs to engage a Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent, applying protocols appropriate for the risk of the portfolio as determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac in its sole discretion. Such value, as so established, shall be
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusive.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For calculation of the Termination Fee, the “Net Servicing Rate” means the Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage less all of the following, expressed as an annualized fractional
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The pass-through rate due Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any Credit Fee in Yield due Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any excess Servicing not retained by the Servicer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any lender-paid mortgage insurance, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other component of the Note Rate that the Servicer is not entitled or
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized to retain as Freddie Mac’s compensation for its Servicing of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any dispute concerning the Termination Fee shall not delay the Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing. Freddie Mac shall deduct the amount of its transfer-related expenses as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 3603.1(a)(i)(A) from the Termination Fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before Freddie Mac’s payment of a Termination Fee, the Servicer must complete all
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchases required under Chapter 3602 and fulfill any other outstanding obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Servicer to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer fails to do so, Freddie Mac will offset the sum of such repurchases and
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other outstanding obligations of the Servicer against the Termination Fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s payment to a Servicer of the Termination Fee will constitute the entire
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensation payable in consideration of the termination of the Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: and related Servicing Contract Rights. A Servicer will not be entitled to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional compensation from Freddie Mac for any reason or cause relating to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: consequential, incidental or indirect damages arising out of, or in connection with, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Transfer of Servicing without cause does not relieve the Servicer of its obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Purchase Documents with respect to the Mortgages related to such Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C) Other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The transfer requirements in Section 3603.2(a), documentation and records
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 3603.3(a) and remittance to Freddie Mac requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3603.3(b) apply to the termination of the Servicing Contract and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract Rights without cause.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding Sections 2301.1(c) through 2301.3, Freddie Mac’s decision to
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspend or disqualify a Seller without cause or to terminate the Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: and related Servicing Contract Rights without cause is conclusive.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Extinguishment of rights in Collateral related to Conveyance or Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Section 3603.1(a)(iii) solely addresses the extinguishment of rights in Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to Conveyance or Financing. Capitalized terms in this Section 3603.1(a)(iii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: correspond with meanings set forth in the Glossary except as otherwise defined in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following exhibits:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit 33A, Acknowledgement Agreement Incorporated Provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit 33B, Acknowledgement Agreement (Syndication) Incorporated Provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit 33C, Acknowledgement Agreement (Cashflow) Incorporated Provisions, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit 33D, Acknowledgement Agreement (Combination) Incorporated Provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon Termination With Cause or Without Cause, any prior approval by Freddie Mac of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conveyance related to Financing—whether characterized as a pledge of the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights or for tax/accounting purposes as a “sale” of cash flow from such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights—is automatically terminated without the need for further action by any person
elements.label: text

elements.text: and does not survive such termination. Upon a Termination With Cause or a Termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without Cause, the Financing’s lender/cashflow “buyer” has no further right, title or
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest whatsoever in the Collateral, with the sole exception of any interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Termination Fee or Surplus Proceeds, if any, and, as applicable, all such rights having
elements.label: text

elements.text: been conclusively extinguished.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale of Mortgages by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac sells Mortgages it has purchased pursuant to the Purchase Documents (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1201.7), Freddie Mac will notify the Servicer upon receipt of an accepted bid and at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least 30 days before the scheduled closing of the purchase. If the acquirer of such Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not require Freddie Mac to engage in a transfer of the Servicing to a Transferee Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: in connection with the sale of the Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer will continue to service the Mortgages on behalf of the acquirer, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will not pay the Servicer any compensation in connection with the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale or any servicing compensation for such Servicing that occurs on or after the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: such sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, however, Servicing is transferred to a Transferee Servicer, then Freddie Mac will pay the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer a Termination Fee that will be calculated in accordance with the requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation of the Termination Fee when the Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights are terminated without cause as described in Section 3603.1(a)(ii) above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements of Sections 3603.2(a), 3603.3(a) and 3603.3(b) apply to a Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing related to the sale of Mortgages by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3603.2: Servicing transfers and compensation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Outstanding purchase commitments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Transfer Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac transfers all or a portion of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights to any Mortgages, the Servicing compensation (as stated in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8105.1(a)) is paid to the Transferee Servicer, except as otherwise set forth in applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents with that Transferee Servicer. Freddie Mac reserves the right to
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiate a Servicing fee with the subsequent Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will have the exclusive right to receive any amount that may be paid by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer for the right to the Servicing compensation under the Servicing Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Outstanding purchase commitments
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the terminated Servicer holds outstanding commitments for the purchase by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: of additional Mortgages, Freddie Mac, in its discretion, may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer the Servicing of those Mortgages to a Transferee Servicer immediately upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: funding the purchases of the Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assign the outstanding commitments to another Seller,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pair off the outstanding commitments, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cancel the outstanding commitments
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3603.3: Documentation and remittance requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documents and records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Documents and records
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, the Servicer is responsible for supplying and (if applicable) causing its Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian to supply all reports, documents and information (including, but not limited to, all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage records specified in Section 8101.4(c)) requested by Freddie Mac on the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The material, including an accounting of the current status of each Mortgage for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights are being terminated, must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared in the form requested by Freddie Mac and delivered to the Transferee Servicer and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new Document Custodian, if applicable, designated by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Remittance to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The remittance to Freddie Mac of Mortgage collections for each Mortgage for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights are terminated must be made on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date specified by Freddie Mac (notwithstanding Freddie Mac’s regular remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, all Escrow Funds, Escrow accounts and prepaid installments held by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer for each Mortgage for which the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights are being terminated must be transferred to the Transferee Servicer on the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must use its best efforts to effect the orderly and efficient Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4101: Uniform Instruments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4101.1: The Mortgage application (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Required use of Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completion instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Required use of Form 65
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 65 must be used for all Mortgage applications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is required to use the version of Form 65 that is current as of the loan application
elements.label: text

elements.text: date. (See Exhibit 4A, Single-Family Uniform Instruments.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller may make changes to the style and formatting of Form 65 and its components –
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Information, Additional Borrower, Continuation Sheet, Lender Loan Information
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Unmarried Addendum, if applicable, in accordance with the UMDP Rendering
elements.label: text

elements.text: Options for the Uniform Residential Loan Application, Document revised 1/2020 (the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Rendering Options”). However, the fields names, descriptions and order of sections may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be altered in any way. Form fields within a section may be moved within that section if
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional field length is needed. Any adjustments made to the format of the form must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: made pursuant to all applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Translation aids for Form 65 and its components are available on Freddie Mac’s Multi￾language Resources for Lenders and Other Housing Professionals webpage. These
elements.label: text

elements.text: translation aids complement the applicable English-language documents and may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided to consumers as supplemental education material when originating single-family
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential Mortgages. The translation aids are for reference only and are not to be executed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Completion instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: A completed Form 65 is used to begin the process of determining the Borrower’s credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputation and capacity to repay the Mortgage. If a residential mortgage credit report
elements.label: text

elements.text: (RMCR) is ordered, the information on Form 65 must be provided to the consumer reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency that is to issue the RMCR. The Seller may elect to complete the liabilities portion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the application directly from the credit reports either manually or through an automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the credit reports identify fewer than three open Tradelines (except for Accept Mortgages),
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller should ask the Borrower if any additional Tradeline references exist (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5202.1(a) for requirements relating to establishing Borrower credit reputation for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manually Underwritten Mortgages)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The final Form 65 must reflect accurate and complete information as of the Note Date. All of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s debts incurred through the Note Date must be included on the final Form 65
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must be considered in the calculation of the Borrower’s monthly debt payment-to-income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio (see Section 5401.2). The final Form 65 must be complete, legible, dated and signed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrowers signing the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information on the initial application must be entered as originally provided by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or, if applicable, as listed on the credit reports, whether handwritten or typed. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: information given by the Borrowers on the application must be consistent with both the
elements.label: text

elements.text: identifying information in the credit reports as well as with the verifications in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file. For any Mortgage in which there is a material discrepancy, the Seller must prepare a
elements.label: text

elements.text: written statement explaining the discrepancy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac agrees that the Seller may receive an initial Form 65 from a Borrower as an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Record (as defined in Section 1401.1(b)) or fax copy. The Freddie Mac Form 65
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains language in the Acknowledgements and Agreements Section in the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information component that permits the Borrower to:

elements.text: 4101.2: Home Mortgage Uniform Instruments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of Uniform Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional Mortgage documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage instruments for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Use of Uniform Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Security Instrument and Note must be executed on the Uniform Instruments (1-4
elements.label: text

elements.text: Family) for the jurisdiction where the Mortgaged Premises is located. The Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments used for a Mortgage must be the versions current as of the Mortgage Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Exhibit 4A, Single-Family Uniform Instruments, for the current dates of revisions of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Instruments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any Mortgage secured by one of the following types of property, Freddie Mac requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: both:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The single-family Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction where the Mortgaged Premises is located, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One of the following types of riders, as applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Required riders by property type
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Type Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit Condominium Rider Form 3140
elements.label: text

elements.text: Planned Unit Development (PUD)
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Planned Unit Development Rider Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1- to 4-unit Investment Property 1- to 4-Family Rider Form 3170
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2- to 4-unit Primary Residence 1- to 4-Family Rider Form 3170
elements.label: text

elements.text: Required riders by property type
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Type Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second home Second Home Rider Form 3890
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Additional Mortgage documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the Uniform Instruments required by this Section 4101.2, certain Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: products have additional loan documentation requirements stated elsewhere in the Guide as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional loan documentation requirements by product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide provision Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages Section 4301.7
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages Section 4402.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes Sections 5703.4(c) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4602
elements.label: text

elements.text: Government Funded, Guaranteed or Insured Mortgages Chapter 4205
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgages Chapter 4502
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage instruments for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: When closing ARMs, the Uniform Instruments used must be based on the applicable ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: product. Below are the required instruments and instructions for completing them.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Required ARM Uniform Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs must be closed using the Uniform Instruments for the applicable ARM product,
elements.label: text

elements.text: consisting of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ARM Note and ARM rider – The Seller must use the most current version of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM Note and ARM rider.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The most current version of the Uniform Instruments is the version in effect as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date of the Mortgage. See Exhibit 4A for the current dates of revisions of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Instruments. See Exhibit 5A, Authorized Changes to Notes, Riders, Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments and the Uniform Residential Loan Application, for authorized changes to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Uniform Instruments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The ARM Note and ARM rider must be completed in accordance with the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable ARM product. See Section 4101.2(c)(iii) for instructions for
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion of Section 4(D) of an ARM Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Security Instrument – The Seller must use the most
elements.label: text

elements.text: current version of the State-specific Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Single Family Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument prepared for use in the jurisdiction in which the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located and the most current version of any applicable property type riders.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certain ARM products, State-specific versions of the ARM Note have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared for Alaska, Florida, New Hampshire, Puerto Rico, Vermont, Virginia, West
elements.label: text

elements.text: Virginia and Wisconsin. If a State-specific version of an ARM Note has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared, it must be used for ARMs secured by Mortgaged Premises located in that
elements.label: text

elements.text: State. The multistate version of the ARM Note, with the required changes stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 5A, must be used in all other States.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac makes available Uniform Instruments for use with ARM products with
elements.label: text

elements.text: various features.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Uniform Instruments have the following features embedded in the form itself (hard￾coded):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Index
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lookback Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assumability
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Uniform Instruments have blanks to be completed by the Seller for the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: features (soft-coded):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maturity Date (determines the term)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ First Interest Change Date (determined by the Initial Period)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum interest rate at the first Interest Change Date (determined by the Initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cap)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum increase or decrease in the interest rate at each adjustment after the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Change Date (Periodic Cap); the Periodic Cap is hard-coded 1% for 30-day
elements.label: text

elements.text: Average SOFR Index (SOFR)-indexed Assumable Life of Loan and Assumable after
elements.label: text

elements.text: Initial Period with 45-day Lookback Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum interest rate for the life of the loan (Lifetime Ceiling, determined by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Life Cap)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum interest rate for the life of the loan (Lifetime Floor, which must equal the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Margin stated in the Note)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In determining which Uniform Instrument to use with each ARM product, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must select the form with the applicable hard-coded information and complete the soft￾coded information as appropriate. Because many of the features of an ARM product are
elements.label: text

elements.text: soft-coded, the same Uniform Instrument may be used for different ARM products.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The 6-Month SOFR-indexed ARM Note and rider may be used with a 3/6-Month, 5/6-
elements.label: text

elements.text: Month, 7/6-Month, and 10/6-Month SOFR-indexed ARM with the same Lookback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period and assumability period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Uniform Instruments may be used for ARM products that do not meet the eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for sale to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac encourages the Seller to use Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments, if available, for originating ARM loans even if the ARM product is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table describe the Uniform Instruments available for different hard-coded
elements.label: text

elements.text: features for ARM products:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Instruments for 6-Month SOFR-indexed ARM products by
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumability period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assumability period Rider Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assumable for life of loan 3141*
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assumable after Initial Period 3142*
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lookback = 45 Days Preceding the Interest Change Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: *Hard-coded with a 1% Periodic Cap
elements.label: text

elements.text: All ARM Uniform Instruments are available on Freddie Mac’s website at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/uniform-instruments/overview.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Use of Fannie Mae ARM instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may use Fannie Mae’s ARM instruments only in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of this subparagraph. The Seller must consult its own legal counsel when
elements.label: text

elements.text: using a Fannie Mae instrument for a particular Freddie Mac ARM product.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller originating ARMs on Fannie Mae instruments represents and warrants that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae instrument, when completed, is appropriate for the applicable Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM product and can be serviced in accordance with the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Instructions for completing Section 4(D) of an ARM Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must complete Section 4(D), Limits on Interest Rate Changes, of an ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Insert in the first blank of the first sentence, referring to the Initial Cap (the maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest rate on the first Interest Change Date), the interest rate that is equal to the sum
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the initial Note Rate for the Mortgage, plus the applicable Initial Cap for that ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: program
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Insert in the second blank of the first sentence, referring to the minimum interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the first Interest Change Date, the interest rate that is equal to the initial Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the Mortgage, minus the applicable Initial Cap. If this difference is less than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Margin stated in the Note, insert the value of the Margin.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Insert in the first blank in the last sentence, referring to the Lifetime Ceiling (the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum interest rate during the life of the Mortgage), the initial Note Rate plus the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Life Cap for that ARM product
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. In the second blank in the last sentence, referring to the Lifetime Floor (the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest rate during the life of the Mortgage), insert the Margin stated in the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4101.3: Master Form and Short Form Security
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Instruments (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Master Form and Short Form Security Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Instructions for use of Master Form Security Instrument; Short Form Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Master Form and Short Form Security Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certain States have statutes that allow originating lenders to record a Master Form Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument in a given county and then to record a Short Form Security Instrument for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent Mortgage originated and recorded in that county. The Short Form Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument contains the loan-specific information (e.g., Borrower name, lender name, loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount, description of property) and identifies the provisions of the Master Form Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument that are being incorporated into the Short Form Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The State-specific Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Master Form Security Instrument and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short Form Security Instrument are available from Freddie Mac’s website at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/uniform-instruments/2021-updated￾instruments. See Exhibit 4A, Single-Family Uniform Instruments, for the list of currently
elements.label: text

elements.text: available Master Form Security Instruments and Short Form Security Instruments and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current dates of these instruments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Mortgages originated on a Short Form Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided the requirements stated in this Section 4101.3 are satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must not use or record a Short Form Security Instrument unless it has already
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded a Master Form Security Instrument in the recorder’s office serving the recording
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction in which the Mortgaged Premises is located. In North Carolina, a Master Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not be recorded in certain designated counties stated in the North Carolina statute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A copy of the recorded Master Form Security Instrument must be provided to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Master Form Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Master Form Security Instrument is the Master Form Mortgage or Deed of Trust that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is permitted under certain State statutes to be recorded by originating lenders in a given
elements.label: text

elements.text: recording jurisdiction in the State. The Master Form Security Instrument for each State
elements.label: text

elements.text: (with the exception of Ohio) consists of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A title page (the name of the title page differs in different States), which contains the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information required under the State statute to record the Master Form Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument in that State and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument for that State
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Ohio, there is no title page; the Master Form Security Instrument is the current Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae/Freddie Mac Ohio Mortgage with the required Master Form information provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the first page of the Ohio Mortgage form itself.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Short Form Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Short Form Security Instrument is the Mortgage or Deed of Trust recorded for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual Mortgage made and recorded in the recording jurisdiction where the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form Security Instrument is recorded.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Short Form Security Instrument contains loan-specific information and incorporates
elements.label: text

elements.text: by reference the provisions of the previously recorded Master Form Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller must not use or record a Short Form Security Instrument unless the Seller has
elements.label: text

elements.text: already recorded a Master Form Security Instrument in the recorder’s office serving the
elements.label: text

elements.text: recording jurisdiction in which the Mortgaged Premises is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Instructions for use of Master Form Security Instrument; Short Form Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Master Form Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Complete the title page by inserting the names and relevant information in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable places and have the title page signed and acknowledged, if required
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Attach the title page to the front of the current Security Instrument for the State
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Record the title page and Security Instrument together in the recorder’s office serving
elements.label: text

elements.text: the recording jurisdiction(s) in which the Seller anticipates subsequently recording
elements.label: text

elements.text: Short Form Security Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. In Ohio, complete the information in the box at the top of the first page of the Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Form by inserting the names and relevant information in the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: places and have the first page signed in the box at the top of the first page. Do not
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete information outside of the box other than “Return to” information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Security Instrument to which the title page is attached (or, in Ohio, on which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Form information is provided) should not have any information completed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: should not be signed or acknowledged.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac revises the Uniform Security Instrument for the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: State, the Seller must record the revised Master Form Security Instrument in all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable local recording offices. The previously recorded Master Form Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument must not be released or superseded.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages originated after the effective date of a revised Uniform Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be originated using a Short Form Security Instrument that references the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded Master Form Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Short Form Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Insert the recording information from the Master Form Security Instrument in the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: paragraph of the “Definitions” section. (The recording information must be that of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Form Security Instrument that was recorded in the recording jurisdiction in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Mortgaged Premises is located.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Insert information for all blank spaces in the “Definitions” section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Check the applicable Rider boxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Insert the property description information
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Provide the Borrower with an exact copy of the recorded Master Form Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument (the completed title page with recordation information and the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: current Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument for the State)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Arrange for the Short Form Security Instrument to be executed by the Borrower(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and acknowledged
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Arrange for any applicable Riders to be executed by the Borrower(s). In
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pennsylvania, attach a copy of the Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Pennsylvania Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage under the caption: “General Provisions Incorporated by Reference and Not
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be Recorded with this Document” after the Rider(s), if any.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Record the Short Form Security Instrument and any applicable Riders in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorder’s office serving the recording jurisdiction in which the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4101.4: Authorized changes to Uniform Instruments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Instrument changes and modifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Addendums or riders
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modifications for Construction Conversion and Renovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Uniform Instrument changes and modifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must not make any changes or additions to the Uniform Instruments that have any
elements.label: text

elements.text: force or effect after purchase of the Mortgage by Freddie Mac, except for changes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: modifications that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Are accomplished by use of Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Instrument Riders
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pertain to an assumption or an alteration of the principal, Note Rate, payment amount or
elements.label: text

elements.text: date, or maturity date of the Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Are required or permitted by the provisions in Exhibit 5A, Authorized Changes to Notes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Riders, Security Instruments and the Uniform Residential Loan Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Addendums or riders
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a change or addition permitted under this section is made to the instrument in the form of
elements.label: text

elements.text: an addendum or rider, the addendum or rider must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be referenced in the instrument it is modifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reference the instrument, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be firmly attached to the instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller agrees that no changes or additions to the Uniform Instruments otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted here will be made if such changes or additions would adversely affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiability of the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Modifications for Construction Conversion and Renovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Uniform Instruments may require modification for use with Construction Conversion
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages. Sellers that use the Uniform Instruments for
elements.label: text

elements.text: construction or renovation loans may make changes or additions necessary for that purpose.
elements.label: text

elements.text: These changes and additions should be provided in a Note addendum or Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument rider. The changes or additions do not need to contain a statement that they
elements.label: text

elements.text: become void upon purchase by Freddie Mac if the changes or additions are necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: assure the first priority of the Mortgage lien or if, by their terms, the changes or additions
elements.label: text

elements.text: cease to be effective before the Settlement Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 4602.3 for information on documenting Construction Conversion
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:

elements.text: ■ Uniform Instrument changes and modifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Addendums or riders
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modifications for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Uniform Instrument changes and modifications

elements.text: force or effect after purchase of the Mortgage by Freddie Mac, except for changes and

elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Modifications for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Uniform Instruments may require modification for use with Construction to Permanent

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages. Sellers that use the Uniform Instruments for
elements.label: text

elements.text: construction or renovation loans may make changes or additions necessary for that purpose.
elements.label: text

elements.text: These changes and additions should be provided in a Note addendum or Security

elements.text: assure the first priority of the Mortgage lien or if, by their terms, the changes or additions

elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Chapter 4602 for information on documenting Construction to Permanent

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages.

elements.text: 4101.5: Availability of Uniform Instruments (03/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Instruments (including condominium rider and Planned Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Development (PUD) rider forms) and Freddie Mac-specific Uniform Instruments are available
elements.label: text

elements.text: from Freddie Mac’s website at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/uniform￾instruments/2021-updated-instruments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may reprint the Uniform Instruments on their own letterhead, by computer or in any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: way that Sellers may choose. However, the text of the Uniform Instruments must remain
elements.label: text

elements.text: unchanged and the tagline that identifies the instrument as a Uniform Instrument must remain
elements.label: text

elements.text: part of the document and be included on each page.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4101.6: Signatures required (07/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Security Instrument must be signed by all individuals with an ownership interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises. (See Exhibit 5A, Authorized Changes to Notes, Riders, Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments, and Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan Application, for permissible changes to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Instruments.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Security Instrument must also be signed by each individual whose signature is necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the applicable statutory or decisional law of the State to create a valid lien, pass clear title,
elements.label: text

elements.text: waive inchoate rights to property or assign earnings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Note or applicable assumption agreement must be signed by any individual whose income or
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial strength is needed to meet the Freddie Mac credit underwriting guidelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is delivered as an owner-occupied Mortgage, the Note must be signed by an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Owner-Occupant.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 5103.5(e) for information on signatures required and forms of signature if
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Borrower is a Living Trust and Section 5103.6(b) for a Land Trust Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4101.7: Seller’s duties regarding Uniform Instruments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Seller agrees that any action taken when enforcing its rights under the Mortgage documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: will not violate the terms of any covenant in the Mortgage documents. The Seller also agrees to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforce its rights under the Uniform Instruments based on instructions or guidance provided by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: of the Security Instrument (12/17/25)

elements.text: The Security Instrument must be properly executed, acknowledged, delivered and recorded in all
elements.label: text

elements.text: places necessary to perfect a First Lien security interest in the Mortgaged Premises in favor of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the mortgagee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee of record, solely as nominee for the lender named in the Security Instrument and the

elements.label: text

elements.text: the lender named in the Security Instrument and the Note, and the lender’s successors and

elements.text: assigns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: MERS registration for Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac.

elements.text: 4101.9: Due-on-sale provisions (07/09/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Description of due-on-sale clauses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Types of due-on-sale clauses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Summary of due-on-sale characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exceptions for applicable laws and certain circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Description of due-on-sale clauses
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “due-on-sale” clauses in the Uniform Instruments include provisions governing whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: the loan will be accelerated if the Borrower sells or transfers the Mortgaged Premises or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in the Mortgaged Premises, or whether instead the loan may be assumed by a third
elements.label: text

elements.text: party who acquires the Mortgaged Premises or any interest in the Mortgaged Premises by sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: or transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Instruments for different Mortgage products may have different due-on-sale clauses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The type of due-on-sale clause set forth in the Uniform Instruments used to originate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: particular Mortgage determines whether the Mortgage is assumable by the party to whom the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is transferred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Uniform Instruments used in connection with the origination of ARMs contain due-on￾sale provisions governing whether the loan will be accelerated if the Borrower sells or
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfers the Mortgaged Premises or any interest in the Mortgaged Premises, or whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: instead the loan may be assumed by a third party who acquires the Mortgaged Premises or
elements.label: text

elements.text: any interest in the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Types of due-on-sale clauses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Due on sale clauses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Type Description
elements.label: text

elements.text: Nonassumable
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the life
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Some due-on-sale clauses provide that the lender will require immediate
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment in full of all sums secured by the Security Instrument (generally
elements.label: text

elements.text: referred to as accelerating the loan) if the Borrower sells or transfers the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The effect of this type of due-on-sale clause is that the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: nonassumable for the life of the loan. The Uniform Instruments used
elements.label: text

elements.text: with fixed-rate Mortgages include this type of due-on-sale clause.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assumable
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the life
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Some due-on-sale clauses state that the lender will not accelerate the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the lender evaluates the proposed sale or transfer as if a new loan were
elements.label: text

elements.text: being made to the intended transferee, and if the lender determines the
elements.label: text

elements.text: lender’s security would not be impaired by the loan assumption.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The effect of this type of due-on-sale clause is that the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumable for the life of the loan. Freddie Mac makes available ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Instruments for all ARM indices that include this type of due￾on-sale clause.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: occurrence of a specified event, but after that specified event has
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurred, the loan is nonassumable.

elements.label: text

elements.text: the loan is assumable provided certain conditions are met. With respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to an ARM, the specified period of time is the end of the Initial Period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs must be assumable during the entire period during which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest rate is adjustable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac makes available ARM Uniform Instruments for 6-Month

elements.text: SOFR-indexed ARMs that include this type of due-on-sale clause, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgages become assumable after the end of the Initial Period when

elements.text: Some due-on-sale clauses include the following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An initial specified period during which the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement that the loan is assumable after the initial specified
elements.label: text
