elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A statement that the loan is nonassumable upon the occurrence of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified event
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Summary of due-on-sale characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-rate Mortgages must be originated as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For all fixed-rate Mortgages other than those listed in the bullet below, on Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments that provide for the Mortgage to be nonassumable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For fixed-rate assumable Mortgages sold under the Guarantor program, on Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments that provide for the Mortgage to be nonassumable and were executed with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Addendum and a Security Instrument Rider that provide for the Mortgage to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumable subject to certain conditions (an “Assumable Mortgage”). The Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Addendum and Security Instrument Rider are available on Freddie Mac’s Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument webpage at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/uniform￾instruments/2021-updated-instruments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval before selling Assumable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac by contacting its Freddie Mac representative or Customer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service at 800-FREDDIE
elements.label: text

elements.text: All 6-Month SOFR-indexed ARMs may be originated on Uniform Instruments that provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: either for the Mortgage to be assumable during the life of the Mortgage or for the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be nonassumable during the Initial Period and assumable thereafter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For eligible Mortgage products that are not originated on Uniform Instruments, such as FHA
elements.label: text

elements.text: and VA Mortgages, the due-on-sale characteristics may be different from those specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Exceptions for applicable laws and certain circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the type of due-on-sale clause stated in the Uniform Instrument used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: originate a Mortgage, Freddie Mac will permit a Mortgage, which under its terms is
elements.label: text

elements.text: nonassumable, to be assumed if required by federal or State law or under the circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: as described in Sections 8406.1(d) and 8406.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Future advances made before purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advances made after purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Future advances made before purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Home Mortgages on which future advances have been made
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Delivery Date, provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The advances have been consolidated with the outstanding principal amount secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage, and the secured principal amount, as consolidated, bears a single interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: rate and repayment term
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lien securing the consolidated principal amount is expressly insured as having First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lien priority by a title insurance policy, by an endorsement to the policy insuring the
elements.label: text

elements.text: consolidated mortgagee’s interest or by other title evidence acceptable to Freddie Mac as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in this Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The consolidated principal amount does not exceed the original loan amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers originating Mortgages on Uniform Instruments that do not contain a covenant
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitting future advances, and who wish to allow future advances, may execute a rider
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitting future advances. The rider must contain language providing that the terms shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: have no force or effect subsequent to a specified date which must be before the sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, in whole or in part, to Freddie Mac. In addition, the rider must contain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following provision:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions, the ability of the lender, its successors and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assigns, to enforce the repayment of future advances made prior to the sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to Freddie Mac shall remain in full force and effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Advances made after purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: On Home Mortgages that have been sold to Freddie Mac, in whole or in part, the Seller or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not make additional advances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4101.11: Origination and delivery of Mortgages using
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: a New York Consolidation, Extension and Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement (the “NY CEMA”) (11/19/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery package for a New York Consolidation, Extension and Modification Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“NY CEMA”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage file documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase refinance Mortgages secured by property located in New York State
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are documented using an NY CEMA. The NY CEMA combines into one set of rights and
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations all of the promises and agreements stated in existing Notes and Mortgages secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises including, if new funds are advanced to the Borrower at the time of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: consolidation, a new Note and Mortgage. The result is that the Borrower has one consolidated
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan obligation, evidenced by a Consolidated Note that is paid in accordance with the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the NY CEMA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following defined terms apply to this section:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to Mortgages using an NY CEMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: O
elements.label: text

elements.text: Original Old Money Note The original Note executed by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is being consolidated, extended and
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified by the newly executed NY CEMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Original New Money (Gap) Note The original Note executed by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the time the current NY CEMA is executed
elements.label: text

elements.text: that represents new funds advanced to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower at the time of the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: consolidation
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with the current financing transaction evidenced by the NY CEMA, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must execute a new original Note, referred to as the Consolidated Note, that
elements.label: text

elements.text: consolidates, extends and modifies the Original Old Money Note and the Original New
elements.label: text

elements.text: Money (Gap) Note, if any.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: When documenting a Mortgage using an NY CEMA, the Seller must use the most current
elements.label: text

elements.text: version of the New York Consolidation, Extension and Modification Agreement Single￾Family Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Instrument (Form 3172). The current version can
elements.label: text

elements.text: be found on Freddie Mac’s website at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/uniform￾instruments/2021-updated-instruments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Borrower must execute a Consolidated Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Delivery package for NY CEMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage originated using the NY CEMA, the Seller must deliver the complete NY
elements.label: text

elements.text: CEMA delivery package listed below to its Document Custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The complete NY CEMA delivery package must include the following documents:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) NY CEMA
elements.label: text

elements.text: The original NY CEMA or a copy certified by the recorder’s office.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) NY CEMA exhibits
elements.label: text

elements.text: Complete set of NY CEMA exhibits, as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit A – A list, or a copy of a list, of the obligations being consolidated, modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: and extended. All Notes, security instruments, assignments, consolidation agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: and related agreements that modify, consolidate or extend prior underlying
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations and which predate the current NY CEMA must be listed separately in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit A.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit B – The property description of the Mortgaged Premises. A copy of Schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: A (Property Description) to the New York Mortgage may be used but should be
elements.label: text

elements.text: marked as Exhibit B to identify it as an NY CEMA exhibit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit C – A copy of the Consolidated Note (including any applicable addenda),
elements.label: text

elements.text: with fixed-rate or adjustable-rate Note language inserted at the top of the first page.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (The required language is stated in (iii) below.) Because this is a form document,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower signatures are not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit D – The most current version of the New York Single-Family Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security Instrument (Form 3033) with all blanks
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed and any applicable riders attached. Because this is a form document,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower signatures are not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) The original Consolidated Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: An original Note executed by the Borrower and endorsed in blank. The Consolidated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note must be the most current version of the applicable Single-Family Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Note with all blanks completed and any applicable addendum
elements.label: text

elements.text: or addenda. The Consolidated Note must have the following language, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: inserted at the top of the document:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Fixed-Rate Notes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consolidated Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Note amends and restates in their entirety, and is given in substitution for, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notes described in Exhibit A of the New York Consolidation, Extension and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Agreement dated the same date as this Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Adjustable-Rate Notes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consolidated Adjustable-Rate Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Note amends and restates in their entirety, and is given in substitution for, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notes described in Exhibit A of the New York Consolidation, Extension and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Agreement dated the same date as this Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Mortgage file documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to Mortgages documented with the NY CEMA, Seller represents and warrants
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Mortgage file contains the documentation required by Sections 3301.2(b), 3302.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4201: General Mortgage Eligibility
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 4201.1: Investment Quality Mortgage (04/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: An Investment Quality Mortgage is a Mortgage that is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Made to a Borrower from whom repayment of the Mortgage can be expected
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adequately secured by real property, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Originated in accordance with the requirements of the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller warrants that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac have the characteristics of an Investment Quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ATR Covered Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac satisfy the QM requirements in the Revised
elements.label: text

elements.text: General QM Rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.2: Mortgage valid First Lien; no prior liens;
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgage not modified (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be a valid First Lien on the Mortgaged Premises. The Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be free and clear of all prior liens and encumbrances, and no rights or condition may exist
elements.label: text

elements.text: that could give rise to such liens, except for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Liens for real estate taxes and special assessments not yet due and payable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rights and conditions specified in Section 4702.4 related to acceptable exceptions to the title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy or to the attorney’s opinion of title letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be a legal, valid and binding obligation of the Borrower, enforceable
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to its terms and conditions and free from any right of setoff, counterclaim or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim or defense. No part of the Mortgaged Premises may have been released from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of the Mortgage may not in any material manner have been modified, amended or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: any way waived or changed, except as permitted by Freddie Mac for Mortgages eligible for sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Construction to Permanent Mortgages or Renovation Mortgages with a modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refinance Mortgages secured by property in New York State that are documented using a
elements.label: text

elements.text: New York Consolidation, Modification and Extension Agreement

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Closed Mortgage requirements

elements.text: (a) Closed Mortgage requirements

elements.text: 4201.5: Principal amount advanced; no mandatory

elements.text: 4201.6: Mortgage not in default (04/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: As of the Freddie Mac Funding Date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ No part of the Borrower’s monthly installment of principal and interest (and, if applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly Escrow payment) may have been 30 days or more delinquent (see definition of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency in the Glossary)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There may not have been any other default under the terms and conditions of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: that remained uncured for 30 days or more after notice of the default to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before the Delivery Date, the Seller, or any prior holder of the Mortgage, must not have, directly
elements.label: text

elements.text: or indirectly, advanced any funds to be used by or on behalf of the Borrower for the payment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal, interest or other charge payable under the terms of the Mortgage.

elements.text: 4201.7: Assumption of Mortgage (04/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A Mortgage not owned by Freddie Mac with respect to which the original Borrower has
elements.label: text

elements.text: conveyed the Mortgaged Premises to a new owner is eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that the new owner:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is obligated by a written assumption agreement to repay the indebtedness secured by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has been fully underwritten and qualified according to the requirements of Topics 5100
elements.label: text

elements.text: through 5500 and any other Guide sections applicable to the Mortgage being assumed. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: example, if the Mortgaged Premises is a Manufactured Home, the provisions of Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703 must be satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.8: Wholesale Home Mortgages (04/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Wholesale Home Mortgages are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section. This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Wholesale Home Mortgages, the Correspondent or Mortgage Broker (or their authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: third parties) may perform all or some of the origination, processing, underwriting, packaging,
elements.label: text

elements.text: funding and/or closing functions described in the Purchase Documents as obligations or
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Seller. Although these functions may not be performed directly by the Seller,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller is responsible for compliance with requirements of all Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must represent and warrant with respect to each Wholesale Home Mortgage it
elements.label: text

elements.text: sells to Freddie Mac that the Seller has management controls in place that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the Mortgage complies with the terms of the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Correspond to the scope and types of risks associated with its wholesale business. Factors
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be considered include, but are not limited to, the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Number of Correspondents and Mortgage Brokers the Seller uses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Length of time the business relationship has been established
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Types of services provided by the Correspondent or Mortgage Broker
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Delivery instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is required to deliver one of the following ULDD Data Points, as applicable, for
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Wholesale Home Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Originator Type of “Correspondent,” or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Originator Type of “Broker”
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.9: General property insurance requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The requirements of Chapters 4703 and 8202 for property and casualty insurance must be met at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time a Mortgage is sold to Freddie Mac and continually thereafter for as long as Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: owns an interest in the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.10: No circumstances adversely affecting value
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: of Mortgage (04/02/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: No proceeding may be pending for condemnation of all or any part of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There may be no circumstances or conditions of which the Seller is aware involving the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, the Mortgaged Premises or the creditworthiness of the Borrower that would adversely
elements.label: text

elements.text: affect the value or marketability of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.11: Mortgages secured by Primary Residences
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupancy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rental income from a Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other Guide provisions
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Occupancy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage is considered to be secured by a Primary Residence when at least one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers occupies all or part of the Mortgaged Premises as a Primary Residence as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Manually Underwritten Mortgages with a loan-to-value ratio greater than 90%, each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower whose income or financial strength was used for qualification purposes must
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupy all or part of the Mortgaged Premises as a Primary Residence as of the Delivery Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage is considered to be secured by a Primary Residence when made to an active￾duty military Borrower who is unable to occupy the Primary Residence prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery Date due to military service when the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage file must contain the Borrower’s military orders verifying the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is temporarily unable to occupy the Primary Residence due to the military
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Seller must deliver ULDD Data Point Investor Feature Identifier valid value
elements.label: text

elements.text: “D76”. See Section 6302.8(a) for more information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage is considered to be secured by a Primary Residence when the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is occupied as a Primary Residence by an individual(s) who is the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: parent(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage is considered to be secured by a Primary Residence when the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is occupied as a Primary Residence by an individual(s) who has a disability and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is their parent or legal guardian
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Rental income from a Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from a Mortgage secured by a Primary Residence is not permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: to qualify the Borrower, except as stated in Section 4501.6 for Home Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Section 5306.1 for rental income from a live-in aide or rental income generated from an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ADU.

elements.text: 4201.12: Second home Mortgages (03/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages secured by second homes are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section. This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Instrument requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following eligibility requirements apply to second home Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be an Accept Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be secured by a 1-unit property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must occupy the second home for some portion of the year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must keep the property available primarily (i.e., more than half of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: calendar year) for the Borrower’s personal use and enjoyment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower may rent the property on a short-term basis provided that the property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not subject to any rental pools or agreements that require the Borrower to rent the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, give a management company or entity control over the occupancy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or involve revenue sharing between any owners and the developer or another
elements.label: text

elements.text: party
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must be in such a location to function reasonably as a second
elements.label: text

elements.text: home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The second home must be suitable for year-round occupancy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: A second home with seasonal limitations on year-round occupancy (e.g., lack
elements.label: text

elements.text: of winter accessibility) is eligible if the appraiser includes at least one comparable sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: with similar seasonal limitations to demonstrate the marketability of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5601.1 for property eligibility requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property must not be subject to any timesharing or other shared ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrangement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s determination of whether a property is a second home is conclusive. A 2-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: property used as a second home is considered an Investment Property and must meet all of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of Section 4201.13.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Maximum number of financed properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Borrower individually and all Borrowers collectively must not be obligated on (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notes, land contracts and/or any other debt or obligation) more than 10 1- to 4-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: financed properties, including the subject property and the Borrower’s Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence. Examples of financed properties that do not have to be counted in these
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitations include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Commercial real estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Multifamily (five or more units) real estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Timeshares
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Undeveloped land
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manufactured homes not titled as real property (chattel lien), unless the property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: situated on the land that is titled as real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property titled in the name of the Borrower’s business if the Borrower, in their
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual capacity, is not obligated on Notes, land contracts and/or any debt or
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation related to such property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property titled in the name of a trust where the Borrower is a trustee if the Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in their individual capacity, is not obligated on Notes, land contracts and/or any debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: or obligation related to such property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Asset eligibility and reserve requirements for second home Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: All funds used to qualify the Borrower for the Mortgage, which includes any funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to be paid by the Borrower and Borrower reserves, must be Borrower personal
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds, as described in Section 5501.3, or other Borrower funds, as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5501.4, except for the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pooled funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gift funds received as a wedding and/or graduation gift
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gifts or grants from an Agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Employer Assisted Homeownership (EAH) Benefits
elements.label: text

elements.text: The reserves requirements in Section 5501.2 must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Rental income from a second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rental income generated from the Borrower’s second home may not be used as stable
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Borrower’s monthly housing expense-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: The monthly housing expense related to a Borrower’s current Primary Residence must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: used in calculating the Borrower’s monthly housing expense-to-income ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Monthly payment amount on the second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: The monthly payment amount (as described in Section 5401.2(b)(8)) on the second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be considered in calculating the Borrower’s monthly debt payment-to-income ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Newly constructed homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchases of newly constructed homes, the Borrower may not be affiliated with or
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the builder, developer or the property seller. For these purposes, “affiliated
elements.label: text

elements.text: with” means that the Borrower may not have an ownership interest in or employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the builder, developer or property seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Uniform Instrument requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 3890, Multistate Second Home Rider, is required for all Mortgages secured by second
elements.label: text

elements.text: homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees, for Credit Fees related to second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the Credit Fee provisions stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 6303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.13: Investment Property Mortgages (03/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Investment Properties are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements of this section. This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Instrument requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery requirements for rental income from all subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following eligibility requirements apply to Investment Property Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be an Accept Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with temporary subsidy buydowns are not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages made to Borrowers who own more than one financed Investment Property are
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, provided that the Investment Property Mortgage being
elements.label: text

elements.text: sold to Freddie Mac is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An eligible fixed-rate, level-payment Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A 7/6-Month or 10/6-Month ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Maximum number of financed properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Borrower individually and all Borrowers collectively must not be obligated on (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notes, land contracts and/or any other debt or obligation) more than 10 1- to 4-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: financed properties, including the subject property and the Borrower’s Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of financed properties that do not have to be counted in these limitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Commercial real estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Multifamily (five or more units) real estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Timeshares
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Undeveloped land
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manufactured homes not titled as real property (chattel lien), unless the property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: situated on the land that is titled as real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property titled in the name of the Borrower’s business if the Borrower, in their
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual capacity, is not obligated on Notes, land contracts and/or any debt or
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation related to such property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property titled in the name of a trust where the Borrower is a trustee if the Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in their individual capacity, is not obligated on Notes, land contracts and/or any debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: or obligation related to such property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reserve requirements for Investment Property Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of whether rental income from the Mortgaged Premises is used in
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualifying, the reserves requirements in Section 5501.2 must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Rental income from an Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5306.1 for requirements related to rental income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Borrower’s monthly housing expense-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: The monthly housing expense related to the Borrower’s current Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be used in calculating the Borrower’s monthly housing expense-to-income ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Asset eligibility limitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: All funds used to qualify the Borrower for the Mortgage, which includes any funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to be paid by the Borrower and Borrower reserves, must be Borrower personal
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds, as described in Section 5501.3, with the exception of pooled funds, which are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not an eligible source of funds for Investment Property Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Newly constructed homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchases of newly constructed homes, the Borrower may not be affiliated with or
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the builder, developer or property seller. For these purposes, “affiliated with”
elements.label: text

elements.text: means that the Borrower may not have an ownership interest in or employment with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: builder, developer or property seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Uniform Instrument requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 3170, 1-4 Family Rider, is required for all Mortgages secured by Investment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Properties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Exhibit 5A, Authorized Changes to Notes, Riders, Security Instruments and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Residential Loan Application, Section VIII, for authorized changes to the 1-4
elements.label: text

elements.text: Family Rider for Investment Property Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delivery requirements for rental income from all subject 1- to 4-unit Investment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of whether rental income from the subject 1- to 4-unit Investment Property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: being used to qualify the Borrower, the Seller must deliver ULDD Data Point Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dwelling Unit Eligible Rent Amount for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject 1-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each unit in a subject 2- to 4-unit Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 6302.8 for delivery requirements for rental income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees, for Credit Fees related to Investment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Mortgages. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the Credit Fee provisions stated
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Chapter 6303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.14: Impact of Contaminated Sites (04/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If the Seller knows of the existence of a Contaminated Site in the Neighborhood where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is located, it must document in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A description of the Contaminated Site
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The location relative to the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller’s opinion on the impact the site may have on whether the Mortgage is an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Investment Quality Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4201.15: Escrow accounts (04/02/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller policy for not requiring Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not require Escrow accounts except with respect to the collection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower-paid mortgage insurance paid monthly as described in Section 4701.2 and when
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Seller policy for not requiring Escrow accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers that sell Mortgages without Escrow accounts must establish and maintain a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy for determining when Escrow accounts are not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s determination that Escrow accounts are not required for a Mortgage must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on the evaluation of the Borrower’s ability to make all payments for the expenses to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid under the Mortgage as they become due. These expenses include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property hazard insurance premiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ground rents and other charges that are or may become First Liens on the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may not waive the requirement for Escrow accounts with respect to collection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower-paid mortgage insurance and when Escrows are required by law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although not required, Freddie Mac encourages Sellers to require Escrows for the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages to Borrowers that are First-Time Homebuyers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Possible® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HomeOne® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Second home Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investment Property Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages where the Borrower has less than six months of reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Refinance Mortgages where taxes were past due on the Mortgage being refinanced

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:

elements.text: (a) Compliance with law
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage and the Servicing of the Mortgage, Mortgage transaction and Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises must comply with all requirements of all applicable federal, State and local
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws, rules and regulations. This includes, but is not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Truth-in-lending laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Licensing laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Doing-business laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Usury laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Anti-predatory lending
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, any right of rescission involving the Mortgage under such laws, rules or
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations must have expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Compliance with QM requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to ensuring compliance with applicable laws, Sellers must ensure that all ATR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Covered Mortgages satisfy the QM requirements of the Revised General QM Rule, even
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the Seller is not required by law or regulation to comply with the Revised General QM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 4202.2(c) for additional information about the ATR/QM Rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Predatory lending practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac actively opposes predatory lending and has implemented a number of policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: designed to combat it. Freddie Mac-approved Seller/Servicers should have policies designed
elements.label: text

elements.text: to identify and avoid predatory lending practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, Sellers must not “steer” a Borrower who qualified for a lower-cost loan product
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a higher-cost loan product. A Seller should offer or direct applicants who seek financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the Seller’s higher-priced subprime or non-prime lending channel toward its standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage line if the applicants qualify for one of the standard products.

elements.text: 4202.2: Specific Mortgage types and regulations

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Home Ownership and Equity Protection Act of 1994 (HOEPA) Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Higher-Priced Mortgage Loans (HPMLs) and Higher-Priced Covered Transactions (HPCTs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ATR/QM Rule eligibility and compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) HOEPA Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Primary Residences, purchase transaction Mortgages and refinance Mortgages that
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed the thresholds under HOEPA and its implementing regulations are ineligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) HPMLs and HPCTs
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with Application Received Dates on or after October 15, 2014, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will purchase HPMLs and HPCTs, as defined in the Glossary, under the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents and this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: HPMLs and HPCTs eligible for sale to Freddie Mac must be one of the following Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Products:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fixed-rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An ARM with an Initial Period of five, seven or 10 years
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: following:

elements.text: (c) ATR/QM Rule eligibility and compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section provides the requirements related to the ATR/QM Rule and specifies which
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages Freddie Mac will purchase and under what conditions those Mortgages will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For ATR Covered Mortgages originated under the GSE Patch, Construction One￾Time Close Construction to Permanent Mortgages with Application Received Dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to July 1, 2021 and Settlement Dates before March 1, 2022 are eligible for sale to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac if originated in accordance with the GSE Patch.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Definitions related to the ATR/QM Rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table contains Glossary definitions related to the ATR/QM Rule and are
elements.label: text

elements.text: incorporated within this section for ease of use:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary definitions related to the ATR/QM Rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: ATR Covered
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ATR Covered Mortgage is a consumer credit transaction secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling that is covered by the ATR/QM Rule and is not an Exempt
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ATR/QM Rule Regulation Z’s Ability to Repay (ATR) rule (12 C.F.R. § 1026.43) that
elements.label: text

elements.text: implements the Truth in Lending Act’s provisions requiring a creditor to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine a Borrower’s ability to repay a Mortgage loan. QMs are a
elements.label: text

elements.text: subset of loans under the ATR requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are two classifications of Exempt Mortgages, as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ TILA Exempt Mortgage: A TILA Exempt Mortgage is a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: exempt from TILA and, as such, is exempt from the ATR
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Regulation Z. TILA Exempt Mortgages include
elements.label: text

elements.text: business purpose loans as defined in 12 C.F.R. § 1026.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ATR Exempt Mortgage: An ATR Exempt Mortgage is a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is exempt from the ATR requirements in Regulation Z as set
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Eligibility requirements for ATR Covered Mortgages and Exempt Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)ATR Covered Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: All ATR Covered Mortgages must meet the requirements of the Revised General
elements.label: text

elements.text: QM Rule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Revised General QM Rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirement reference summary1
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: reference2
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1026.43(e)(2)(i) Mortgage must be fully amortizing with regular periodic
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments that are substantially equal and that do not result in
elements.label: text

elements.text: an increase of the principal balance or allow the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: defer repayment of principal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1026.43(e)(2)(ii) Mortgage term cannot exceed 30 years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1026.43(e)(2)(iii) Mortgage must not exceed the applicable points and fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitations specified in the Revised General QM Rule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1026.43(e)(2)(iv) The monthly payment for Mortgage-related obligations must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be taken into account when underwriting the Mortgage using:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maximum interest rate that may apply during the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: five years after the date on which the first regular periodic
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment will be due
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Periodic payments of principal and interest that will repay
elements.label: text

elements.text: the loan amount over the loan term
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1026.43(e)(2)(v) Mortgage must meet consider and verify requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in the ATR/QM Rule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1026.43(e)(2)(vi) Mortgage must not equal or exceed the applicable APR-APOR
elements.label: text

elements.text: QM spread thresholds specified in the Revised General QM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rule.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ATR/QM Rule, including the Revised General QM Rule requirements.

elements.text: (B) Exempt Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exempt Mortgages are not subject to the Revised General QM Rule; however, they
elements.label: text

elements.text: must meet:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Points and fees limitations set forth below for Exempt Mortgages; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annual percentage rate (APR)/average prime offer rate (APOR) threshold
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitations set forth below for Exempt Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers are responsible for determining whether the Mortgage is an Exempt Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Points and fees limitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Points and fees limitations for ATR Covered Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ATR Covered Mortgages with points and fees exceeding 3% of the total loan amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or such other applicable limits for lower-balance Mortgages) as specified under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Truth in Lending Act and its implementing regulations, 12 C.F.R. 1026.43(e)(3), will
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac. Sellers must use the points and fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation that is required for QMs under the Truth in Lending Act and its
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementing regulations that are found at 12 C.F.R. 1026.43(e)(3) and 12 C.F.R. §
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1026.32(b) to determine compliance with applicable requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Points and fees limitations for Exempt Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exempt Mortgages will be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if the following conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such Mortgages are exempt from Regulation Z, 12 C.F.R. § 1026.43(a) (ATR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exempt Mortgages) or are not subject to the Truth in Lending Act (TILA Exempt
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The points and fees do not exceed 5% of the total loan amount. Sellers must use
elements.label: text

elements.text: the points and fees calculation that is required for high-cost Mortgages under
elements.label: text

elements.text: HOEPA and its implementing regulations, 12 C.F.R. § 1026.32(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) APR-APOR threshold limitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Permissible spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: The permissible spread between the APR and the APOR on the date the interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: is set depends on ATR coverage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ATR Covered Mortgages: The Mortgage’s APR may not exceed the APOR by
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2.25 percentage points or more or such other applicable threshold as specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single Family Seller/Servicer Guide Chapter 4202
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Revised General QM Rule for ATR Covered Mortgages, calculated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the provisions of that rule; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exempt Mortgages: The Mortgage’s APR may not exceed the APOR by 6.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentage points or more for TILA Exempt and ATR Exempt Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated in accordance with the provisions of the ATR/QM Rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Short-term ARMs QM APR Calculation
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all ARMs with an initial fixed period of five years or less that are ATR Covered
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, Sellers must calculate the APR in accordance with the Revised General
elements.label: text

elements.text: QM Rule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When calculating the APR for APR-APOR threshold purposes for 3/6-Month
elements.label: text

elements.text: SOFR and 5/6-Month SOFR ARMs that are ATR Covered Mortgages, Sellers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculate the APR using the maximum interest rate that may apply during the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: five years after the date on which the first regular periodic payment will be due.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Term – Interim Construction Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers must structure any Interim Construction Financing to be exempt from the ability
elements.label: text

elements.text: to repay provisions of Regulation Z, 12 C.F.R. § 1026.43(a)(3) so that the Mortgage term
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not exceed 30 years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Life of loan representation and warranty
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers are responsible for representations and warranties related to requirements set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Section 4202.2(c) for the life of the loan, and Freddie Mac’s rights to

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single Family Seller/Servicer Guide Chapter 4202
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with private transfer fee covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mandatory arbitration
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Credit insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not purchase or securitize any Mortgage if the Borrower obtained a prepaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: single-premium credit-life, credit disability, credit unemployment or credit property
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy in connection with the origination of the Mortgage, regardless of whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: the premium was financed in the Mortgage amount or paid from the Borrower’s funds. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: prohibition does not apply to credit insurance products where premiums are calculated,
elements.label: text

elements.text: earned and paid on a monthly or other regular periodic basis or to prepaid hazard, flood or
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance policies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Mortgages with private transfer fee covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages on properties encumbered by private transfer fee covenants prohibited by 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: C.F.R. Part 1228 are ineligible for purchase by Freddie Mac if those covenants were created
elements.label: text

elements.text: on or after February 8, 2011.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Seller/Servicer represents and warrants that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It has controls in place to ensure that it does not inadvertently deliver an ineligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to Freddie Mac as described above, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If applicable, it has received representations and warranties from any person or entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: from which the Seller purchased the Mortgage that the property securing the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not encumbered by private transfer fee covenants created on or after February 8, 2011
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, private transfer fees are permissible if all of the following conditions are met for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage secured by a property encumbered by a private transfer fee:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is a shared equity loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage meets the Duty to Serve shared equity loan program criteria identified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12 CFR 1282.34(d)(4) (other than the Duty to Serve 100% of Area Median Income limit)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage has a Note Date on or after July 1, 2023
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mandatory arbitration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not purchase any Mortgage if any of the Mortgage documents—including
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Note, any Note addendum, the Security Instrument or any Security Instrument rider—
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain a “mandatory arbitration” clause (i.e., a clause that obligates the Borrower to submit
elements.label: text

elements.text: to arbitration any dispute arising out of or relating in any way to the Mortgage transaction).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Single Family Seller/Servicer Guide Chapter 4202
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Uniform Instruments do not provide for mandatory arbitration, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition of a mandatory arbitration clause is not an authorized change to the Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instruments. No ancillary Mortgage document may contain a mandatory arbitration
elements.label: text

elements.text: provision.

elements.text: (i) Rounding requirements

elements.text: To determine whether a Mortgage complies with the maximum LTV, TLTV and
elements.label: text

elements.text: HTLTV ratio requirements, the calculated ratio must be rounded up to the next
elements.label: text

elements.text: whole number. For example, 94.01% must be rounded up to 95%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At delivery, Freddie Mac will calculate the LTV, TLTV and HTLTV ratios for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage based on data delivered by the Seller. Each ratio will be calculated to two
elements.label: text

elements.text: decimal places, and the result will be rounded up to the next whole number.

elements.text: Chapter 4204: Secondary Financing and Other
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Financing Arrangements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4204.1: Mortgages with secondary financing (03/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for all secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements for new secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements for existing secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees and other delivery instructions for Mortgages with secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Requirements for all secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secondary financing means any financing that is subordinate in lien priority to the First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. Freddie Mac will purchase First Lien Mortgages with secondary financing under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the Purchase Documents and this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of any secondary financing must be disclosed to the appraiser and to the MI. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing terms that must be disclosed include the Note Rate and the name of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: institution or individual providing the financing. The Seller may not indicate a value needed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: support the transaction, or provide to the appraiser any information about an expected loan-to￾value (LTV) ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as specifically stated in Section 4204.2 with respect to Affordable Seconds®, the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing must not permit the provider or any other party to share in the appreciation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises (equity sharing).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Secondary financing is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For special requirements for Affordable Seconds, refer to Section 4204.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Additional requirements for new secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for new secondary financing (i.e., the First Lien Mortgage and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing originated on the same day)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amortization
elements.label: text

elements.text: period
elements.label: text

elements.text: The amortization period for the secondary financing must run for at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least five years after the Note Date, unless the secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: is fully amortizing or is a Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Call provision The secondary financing must not contain a call provision that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: exercisable within the first five years after the Note Date, unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing is a HELOC.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maturity date The maturity date of the secondary financing must be at least five
elements.label: text

elements.text: years after the Note Date, unless the secondary financing is fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: amortizing or a HELOC.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of the secondary financing must provide for regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payments that cover at least the interest that accrues during
elements.label: text

elements.text: the previous month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must include a copy of the following documentation in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note or other evidence of secondary financing terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement or an alternative form
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by law that evidences the fees and costs related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing paid by the Borrower at closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For HELOCs, the HELOC agreement showing all fees and costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid by the Borrower at closing, and the maximum permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit advance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 4302.5 for special requirements for Refi
elements.label: text

elements.text: Possible® Mortgages with new secondary financing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Employer Assisted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homeownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (EAH) Benefit
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the secondary financing is an EAH Benefit:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The terms of the secondary financing must not require
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment in full unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower terminates their employment for any reason,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The employer terminates the Borrower’s employment for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any reason other than the Borrower’s long-term disability,
elements.label: text

elements.text: elimination of the Borrower’s position or a reduction-in￾force
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the monthly payment may be excluded from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly housing expense-to-income ratio and the monthly debt
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment-to-income ratio when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The monthly payments of principal and interest or interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: only begin on or after the due date of the 61st monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment under the First Lien Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Repayment of principal is due only upon sale or default
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Additional requirements for existing secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase First Lien refinance Mortgages with existing secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including HELOCs) that is not paid off from the proceeds of the refinance Mortgage if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller includes in the Mortgage file evidence of the subordination of all outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: secondary financing, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The secondary financing has scheduled payments sufficient to meet the interest due
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees, for Credit Fees related to Mortgages with secondary
elements.label: text

elements.text: financing. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the Credit Fee provisions stated in Chapter
elements.label: text
