elements.text: 4204.2: Affordable Seconds®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special requirements for Affordable Seconds®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special requirements for Affordable Seconds secured by properties subject to equity sharing
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special delivery instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Special requirements for Affordable Seconds®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Seconds must comply with the requirements of Section 4204.1(a) and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section, regardless of whether the Affordable Second is originated
elements.label: text

elements.text: simultaneously with the First Lien Mortgage (i.e., the First Lien Mortgage and the Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second are originated on the same day) or is being subordinated to the First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: through a refinance transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A checklist for Affordable Seconds is available as an additional resource at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/docs/pdf/affordable-seconds-checklist.pdf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The special requirements in Section 4204.2(b) must be met if an Affordable Second is
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by a property that is subject to an equity sharing agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Seconds secured by Manufactured Homes or CHOICEHomes® must meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional requirements in Sections 5703.5(c) and 5703.12(g).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 4504.5 for special requirements for HeritageOne® Mortgages with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Seconds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Funding source
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Affordable Second must not be funded in any way through the First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction, including differential pricing in rate, discount points or fees for individual
elements.label: text

elements.text: loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may not participate in an equity sharing agreement with respect to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, unless the equity sharing agreement meets the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4204.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Affordable Second must not be funded by the property seller or another interested
elements.label: text

elements.text: party to the transaction, except as permitted in Section 4204.2(a)(i)(B), Section 4406.10
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4502.3(a) for Community Land Trust Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms and conditions of the Seller’s secondary financing or financial assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: program must be made available by the Seller to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Non-Seller-funded Affordable Seconds
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Affordable Second must be provided by an Agency, a credit union or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: community development financial institution (CDFI) pursuant to an established,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ongoing, documented secondary financing or financial assistance program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to the subject Mortgage, an Agency or credit union must not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be the Seller or have participated in any aspect of the Mortgage origination
elements.label: text

elements.text: process, other than to assess the Borrower’s ability to meet the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the program and to fund the Affordable Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be affiliated with, under contract to, or financed (directly or indirectly) by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller or by any party that participated in the Mortgage origination process such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as the property seller, builder, developer or real estate agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to the subject Mortgage, a CDFI working with an Agency must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a CDFI that has received CDFI certification from the Treasury’s CDFI Fund (a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Certified CDFI”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remain a Certified CDFI through the date the Mortgage is sold to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Agency with which the CDFI is working may participate in the origination of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Agency may be affiliated with, under contract to or financed by the Certified
elements.label: text

elements.text: CDFI if the Agency is a nonprofit entity and third-party originator that works with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certified CDFI.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For these purposes, “affiliated with” means that the Agency and the Seller or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: party are related to each other as a consequence of one entity directly or indirectly
elements.label: text

elements.text: controlling the other party, being controlled by the other party or being under
elements.label: text

elements.text: common control with that party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The First Lien Mortgage must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fixed-rate Mortgage or an ARM with an initial fixed-rate period of five years or
elements.label: text

elements.text: more,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A purchase transaction or a “no cash-out” refinance, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by a 1-4 unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Seller-funded Affordable Seconds
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller may be the source of an Affordable Second if the following requirements are
elements.label: text

elements.text: met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller is not a CDFI, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be a depository institution
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Have an Affordable Seconds program that supports its affordable lending
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or mission focused program(s), or a program designed to broadly support
elements.label: text

elements.text: its Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller is a credit union that is not required by law to participate in the CRA,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the credit union must have an Affordable Seconds program that supports its
elements.label: text

elements.text: affordable lending and/or mission-focused program
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Seller is a CDFI, the Seller may be a non-depository institution if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: established Affordable Seconds program supports affordable lending and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit needs of prospective homebuyers in the Seller’s market(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The First Lien Mortgage must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Home Possible® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A fixed-rate Mortgage or an ARM with an initial fixed-rate period of five
elements.label: text

elements.text: years or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An Accept Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A purchase transaction Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Secured by a 1-unit Primary Residence, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Retail Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Maturity date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of the Affordable Second must not require a balloon payment that becomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: due before the maturity date or before payment in full of the First Lien Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Affordable Second is an Employer Assisted Homeownership Benefit, the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the secondary financing must not require immediate repayment in full, except when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower terminates their employment for any reason, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The employer terminates the Borrower’s employment for any reason other than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s long-term disability, elimination of the Borrower’s position or a reduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: in force
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Scheduled payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Affordable Second may be forgivable or repayable. The interest rate of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Second must not be more than 2% higher than the interest rate of the First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lien Mortgage. Accrued interest that is added to principal may not at any time during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: term of the First Lien Mortgage increase the total loan-to-value (TLTV) ratio beyond the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum TLTV ratio allowed for the First Lien Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If monthly payments on the Affordable Second are required and begin before the due date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the 61st monthly payment under the First Lien Mortgage, such monthly payments must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be included in the Borrower’s monthly housing expense-to-income ratio and monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt payment-to-income ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If monthly payments on the Affordable Second begin on or after the due date of the 61st
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment under the First Lien Mortgage, or if repayment of the Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second is due only upon sale or default, the amount of the Affordable Second monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment may be excluded from both ratios.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Financing structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Affordable Second must not be a Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must retain in the Mortgage file the following documentation for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Second:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Note or other evidence of the terms of the Affordable Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement or an alternative form required by law
elements.label: text

elements.text: that evidences the fees and costs related to the Affordable Second paid by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower at closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For refinance transactions, evidence of the subordination of any existing Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Affordable Seconds proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Proceeds from the Affordable Second may be used toward the Down Payment or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Closing Costs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Special requirements for Affordable Seconds secured by properties subject to equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: sharing agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For-profit entities may not share in the appreciation of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the terms of an Affordable Second permit the Agency or subsidy provider to share in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appreciation of the Mortgaged Premises, the following requirements must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At the time of the origination of the Affordable Second, the share of appreciation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Agency or subsidy provider is entitled, expressed as a percentage, must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed the original principal amount of the Affordable Second divided by value as
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with Section 4203.1 (“the percentage of the Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second”), except as stated in the next bullet.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the original principal amount of the Affordable Second is equal to 5% of
elements.label: text

elements.text: value, the percentage of the Affordable Second is 5%, and the maximum share of
elements.label: text

elements.text: appreciation the Agency or subsidy can receive is 5%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The share of appreciation to which the Agency or subsidy provider is entitled may exceed
elements.label: text

elements.text: the percentage of the Affordable Second if the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Agency or subsidy provider must not charge interest on the Affordable Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The share of appreciation must not exceed 75%, unless the Affordable Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: provider is a subsidy provider or program administrator managing an income-based
elements.label: text

elements.text: resale restriction program and the Seller confirms that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The special requirements for Mortgages secured by properties subject to income￾based resale restrictions in Chapter 4406 are met, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subsidy provider or program administrator has processes in place to allow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to receive a share of the proceeds of a subsequent sale of the property in
elements.label: text

elements.text: instances where the resale sales price is higher than the subsidized sales price paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Borrower to purchase the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The terms of the Affordable Second must allow the Borrower to recover all the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Agency or subsidy provider is able to share in the appreciation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any portion of the Down Payment paid from Borrower funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Customary costs incurred by the Borrower in selling the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Payments of principal made under the First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The right of the Agency or subsidy provider to share in the appreciation must be clearly
elements.label: text

elements.text: subordinate to the First Lien Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Special delivery instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 6302.34(b)(iv) for special delivery instructions for Mortgages with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Affordable Seconds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4204.3: Temporary subsidy buydown plans (04/12/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special underwriting requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special documentation requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Temporary subsidy buydown plans (“buydown plans”) allow the Borrower to benefit from a
elements.label: text

elements.text: temporary subsidy of the monthly payment of principal and interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Limited Buydown Mortgages and Extended Buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages pursuant to the terms of the Purchase Documents and this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Limited Buydown Mortgage, the initial interest rate must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Temporarily reduced to no more than two percentage points below the Note Rate, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Increased by no more than one percentage point annually for no more than two years
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the first scheduled payment date
elements.label: text

elements.text: For an Extended Buydown Mortgage, the initial interest rate must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Temporarily reduced by more than two percentage points, but no more than three
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentage points, below the Note Rate, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Increased by no more than one percentage point annually for more than two years, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: no more than three years, from the first scheduled payment date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible® Mortgages and HeritageOne® Mortgages with buydown plans must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this section and Sections 4501.2 and 4504.5, respectively.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages are not eligible for buydown plans:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 3/6 Month ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 5/6-Month ARMs secured by a 3-to 4-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash-out refinance Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “No cash-out” refinance Mortgages with a buydown plan funded with a lender credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: derived from an increase in the interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investment Property Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any Mortgage with a buydown plan, the initial interest rate may not be more than three
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentage points below the Note Rate. The buydown plan may not extend for more than
elements.label: text

elements.text: three years after the first scheduled payment date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Special underwriting requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Establishing housing and debt payment-to-income ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: For fixed-rate Mortgages, the Seller must qualify the Borrower using monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments calculated at the Note Rate; however, the interest rate used to qualify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must be equal to or greater than the maximum interest rate that may apply
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the first five years after the date on which the first regular periodic payment will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be due, based on the loan amount over the term of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For ARMs, the Seller must qualify the Borrower using monthly payments calculated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 4401.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Calculation of reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: If reserves are required, the reserves must be calculated using the Note Rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Special documentation requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage file must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the executed buydown agreement, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller’s calculations of the total cost of the buydown plan, any interested party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contributions and the annual percentage increase in the Borrower’s monthly principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payment during the buydown period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The buydown agreement must provide that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The funds in the buydown account will be automatically applied each month to reduce
elements.label: text

elements.text: the monthly payment of principal and interest;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If, for any reason, the buydown funds are not available or the buydown funds are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid, the Borrower will remain obligated to make the full monthly Mortgage payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: required under the terms of the Note; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower will not assign, transfer or close the buydown account, or withdraw
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown funds, except as permitted by the terms of the buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Other requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Custodial Account requirements for buydowns
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each buydown plan must be fully funded at origination. See Sections 8302.1(a)(ii) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.2(b) for Custodial Account requirements for buydown plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Note and Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: No references to the buydown plan are permitted in the Note and Security Instruments. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: no event may the buydown agreement change the terms of the Note or the Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The interest rate and monthly payments shown in the Note must be calculated without
elements.label: text

elements.text: reference to the buydown plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is foreclosed, the funds in the buydown account must be used to reduce
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage debt. If the Mortgage is paid in full, the funds must be distributed in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the buydown agreement. If the property is sold and the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumed by the purchaser, the funds may continue to be used to reduce the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments in accordance with the original terms of the buydown agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Delivery requirements for Mortgages with buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 6302.18 for information on the delivery and pooling requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with a buydown plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4204.4: Shared equity plans (05/07/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not approve individual shared equity plans; Freddie Mac will, however,
elements.label: text

elements.text: consider purchasing Mortgages subject to individual shared equity plans if all of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must obtain a Mortgage application, financial statements and credit reports for
elements.label: text

elements.text: both the Owner-Occupant and the owner-investor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All requirements in the Seller’s Purchase Documents must be satisfied
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The shared equity plan must be a written agreement and the Seller must retain a copy in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller warrants the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Owner-Occupant made a Down Payment of at least 5% from Borrower personal funds as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5501.3(k)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The owner-investor made an additional Down Payment of at least 5%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Both the Owner-Occupant and the owner-investor signed the Note and Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Both the Owner-Occupant and the owner-investor are individuals (i.e., not corporations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited partnerships, partnerships or trusts)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ No agreement requiring the sale of the property or the buyout of either owner’s interest may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be in effect during the first seven years after the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Neither the property seller nor any other interested party to the transaction (such as a builder,
elements.label: text

elements.text: a real estate broker or a real estate agent) is a party to the shared equity plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer is able to service the Mortgage according to the requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: or insured Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: USDA Rural Housing Service (RHS) (12/03/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:

elements.text: ■ Overview and defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special RHS documentation instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage file contents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special Seller warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview and defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 502 GRH Mortgages guaranteed by the United States Department of Agriculture
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac in accordance with the provisions of this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval to sell Section 502 GRH Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: by contacting its Freddie Mac representative or the Contact Support Center at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Glossary definitions for Section 502 GRH Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller should be familiar with the Glossary definitions of the following terms:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary definitions for Section 502 GRH Mortgages

elements.text: agency.

elements.text: The following terms apply for purposes of this section:

elements.text: Each Section 502 GRH Mortgage must be originated in compliance with all of the

elements.text: requirements of the Guaranteed Regulations and the Lender Agreement, unless these
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modified or waived in writing by RHS, and Freddie Mac has approved those
elements.label: text

elements.text: modifications or waivers, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Specifically restricted or modified by this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Section 502 GRH Mortgage must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An assumable or nonassumable First Lien secured by a Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A purchase transaction or “no cash-out” refinance of existing Section 502 GRH Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 30-year fully amortizing fixed-rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Non-Loan Product Advisor® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 502 GRH Mortgages must be sold to Freddie Mac with recourse, within the meaning
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Section 6201.2(b)(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Loan limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: No Section 502 GRH Mortgage may have an original principal balance in excess of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum principal loan amount allowed under the RHS Guaranteed Regulations or in excess
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the maximum loan amounts for conventional Mortgages stated in Section 4203.1(c),
elements.label: text

elements.text: whichever is less. A Section 502 GRH Mortgage may not be a super conforming Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Section 502 GRH Mortgage must comply with the provisions of the Guide, except that
elements.label: text

elements.text: if there are Guaranteed Regulations applicable to the following matters, the Guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulations will apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financing concessions and temporary subsidy buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan-to-value (LTV) and total LTV (TLTV) ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sources of funds for Down Payment, Closing Costs and reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Section 502 GRH Mortgage must comply with the Guaranteed Regulations for
elements.label: text

elements.text: LTV/TLTV ratios, and the LTV/TLTV ratios must not exceed 115%, as calculated under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 4203.1(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Special RHS documentation instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Loan Guarantee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 502 GRH Mortgages are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac prior to the receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Loan Guarantee if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ RHS has issued its Conditional Commitment and the conditions precedent to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: issuance of the Loan Guarantee stated in the Guaranteed Regulations and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conditional Commitment have been satisfied
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has delivered the Guaranteed Loan Closing Report, current Form RD
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1980-19, to RHS and has paid the RHS Loan Guarantee fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As of the Delivery Date, the Seller represents and warrants that there is no basis for
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS to fail to issue the Loan Guarantee and that it has not been notified by RHS that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Loan Guarantee has been denied
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Lender Record Change
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must complete and execute the Lender Record Change and submit it to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate RHS Servicing Office within 15 calendar days after the Delivery Date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Section 502 GRH Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of completing the Lender Record Change, the Seller is the “Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender,” Freddie Mac is the “Purchasing (Holding) Lender,” and the Seller is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Lender’s Servicing Agent.” Freddie Mac’s lender identification number is 52-0904874.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must leave the “Purchasing Lender Agency Assigned Branch No.” blank.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Mortgage file contents
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the documents required in Chapters 3301 and 3401, the Mortgage file for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 502 GRH Mortgage must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Conditional Commitment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Lender Record Change
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Loan Guarantee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Special Seller warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller warrants to Freddie Mac, as of the Delivery Date of each Section 502 GRH
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has obtained a determination from RHS that the Seller is eligible to participate
elements.label: text

elements.text: as an originating lender for Section 502 GRH Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has executed the Lender Agreement that is and will remain in full force and
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Loan Guarantee by RHS of the Section 502 GRH Mortgage is in full force and in
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect as of the Delivery Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has complied and will continue to comply with the RHS Guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulations, the RHS Lender Agreement and all other applicable RHS guidelines,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Mortgage instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Section 502 GRH Mortgage is assumable, it must be originated on Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: instruments approved by the FHA for FHA-insured assumable fixed-rate Mortgages with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable riders and addenda required by the FHA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Section 502 GRH Mortgage is not assumable, it must be originated on the then-current
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Instruments, including all applicable riders required in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4101.2 for the jurisdiction in which the Mortgaged Premises are located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all Section 502 GRH Mortgages, the monthly payment due date stated in the Note must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be the first day of the month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Delivery instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.15 for delivery and pooling requirements for Section 502 GRH Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: sold to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees, for Credit Fees related to Section 502 GRH
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the Credit Fee provisions stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 6303.

elements.text: (a) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Home Possible® Mortgage with an RHS Leveraged Second is eligible for sale to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac in accordance with the provisions of this chapter. Freddie Mac will not purchase the
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS Leveraged Second.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following additional terms apply for the purposes of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: S
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second Loan Documentation Second Loan Documents are all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following RHS documents:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Promissory Note, Form RD 1940-16 (Rev. 10-
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real Estate [Deed of Trust] or [Mortgage] for
elements.label: text

elements.text: [the State in which the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located], Form RHS 3550-14 (Rev. 10-96)

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: leveraged Mortgages originated by RHS contained
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Part 3550 of Title 7 of the Code of Federal

elements.label: text

elements.text: may be modified by RHS from time to time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Home Possible Mortgage purchased by Freddie Mac must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A First Lien secured by a 1-unit Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A purchase transaction Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 30-year fully amortizing fixed-rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Maximum LTV and TLTV ratio limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum LTV ratio of the Home Possible Mortgage with the RHS Leveraged Second is
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The maximum LTV value of the Home Possible Mortgage does not include the RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Leveraged Second.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum TLTV ratio of the Home Possible Mortgage with the RHS Leveraged Second,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which includes the RHS Leveraged Second and all other secondary financing, is the lesser of
elements.label: text

elements.text: 95% or the maximum allowed under the Second Loan Regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) RHS requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must comply with all Freddie Mac requirements for Home Possible Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may allow the Borrower underwriting and qualification flexibilities with Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Possible Mortgages that are not permitted for the first Mortgage under the Second Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the following areas, the Seller must comply with the provisions of the Second Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulations pertaining to the first Mortgage even in situations where the underwriting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualification requirements for Home Possible Mortgages are more flexible:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Amount and source of funds for the Down Payment and Closing Costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower income limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Homeownership education
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum allowable qualifying ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rural designation as defined by RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Temporary subsidy buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing concessions, if used as a source of funds, must be limited to such Closing Costs as
elements.label: text

elements.text: are customary in the market where the Mortgaged Premises are located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Additional documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to documentation required for the Home Possible Mortgage, the Mortgage file for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages with an RHS Leveraged Second must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Borrower’s Certification of Eligibility, Form RD 1944.59 (Rev. 10-96)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copies of the executed Second Loan Documents and any other documents executed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower in connection with the RHS Leveraged Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Special Seller warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller warrants that the RHS Leveraged Second:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has been closed and all proceeds advanced to the Borrower or at the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: designation, prior to the Delivery Date of the Home Possible Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At all times after the Note Date of the Home Possible Mortgage, the debt obligation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: lien or other restriction on the Mortgaged Premises, of the RHS Leveraged Second and
elements.label: text

elements.text: any recorded Second Loan Document are subordinate to the Home Possible Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Was originated on the Second Loan Documents. Any changes or modifications to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Second Loan Documents must be approved by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Was originated by RHS pursuant to the Second Loan Regulations in effect on the closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the RHS Leveraged Second. Any waivers or modifications to the RHS Second
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Regulations with respect to an individual Borrower must be approved by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Was originated by a Seller approved by RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Borrower must be underwritten and approved by the RHS Field Office for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS Leveraged Second.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Delivery instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.14 for delivery and pooling requirements for Home Possible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: with RHS Leveraged Seconds sold to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Credit Fees and Credit Fee Caps related to Home Possible Mortgage with an RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Leveraged Second, the Seller must refer to Exhibit 19, Credit Fees, and Exhibit 19A, Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fee Cap Eligibility Criteria. Credit Fees are paid in accordance with the Credit Fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions stated in Chapter 6303.

elements.text: (a) Overview

elements.text: Section 184 Native American Mortgages are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac in accordance

elements.text: Mortgages by contacting its Freddie Mac representative or the Contact Support Center at

elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Glossary definitions for Section 184 Native American Mortgages

elements.text: The Seller should be familiar with the Glossary definitions of the following terms:

elements.text: Glossary definitions for Section 184 Native American Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: G
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: American Mortgages

elements.label: text

elements.text: originated pursuant to the Section 184 Regulations and the

elements.text: terms of the Guide. The related ULDD valid value is Public

elements.text: And Indian Housing.

elements.text: (ii) Other terms used in the section
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following terms apply for purposes of this section:

elements.label: text

elements.text: I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Indian Any person recognized as being Indian or Alaska Native by an

elements.text: Indian Tribe, the federal government or any State and includes

elements.text: the term “Native American”.

elements.label: text

elements.text: recipient under Chapter 67 of Title 31, of the U.S. Code.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages and ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Section 184 Native American Mortgage must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a purchase transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a fully amortizing, fixed-rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be assumable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an original UPB within the maximum loan amount allowed under the Section 184
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulations or the maximum loan amount for conventional Mortgages stated in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4203.1(c), whichever is less
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a Non-Loan Product Advisor® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Section 184 Native American Mortgage must not be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Originated in connection with other FHA and VA programs, such as Section 203(k) of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the National Housing Act
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A super conforming Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Community Land Trust Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage secured by a property subject to income-based resale restrictions that
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate upon foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable legally required foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: redemption period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, where the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: value must be determined in accordance with Section 4406.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Section 184 Native American Mortgage must be sold to Freddie Mac with recourse,
elements.label: text

elements.text: within the meaning of Section 6201.2(b)(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Compliance with Freddie Mac’s requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Section 184 Native American Mortgage must comply with the provisions of the Guide,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except that if there are Section 184 Regulations applicable to the following matters, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 184 Regulations apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisals
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interested party contributions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan-to-value (LTV) and total LTV (TLTV) ratios
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage and property insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secondary financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sources of funds for the Down Payment and Closing Costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Temporary subsidy buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Title status report
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the title to the Mortgaged Premises is an unrestricted fee simple, the provisions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4702 apply. When title to the Mortgaged Premises is other than unrestricted fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: simple, the Seller must obtain a title status report and any other documentation required by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Section 184 Regulations. The Seller must record the Security Instrument, and all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage documents required to be recorded, in accordance with the terms of the Section 184
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Mortgage file documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the documents required by Chapters 3301 and 3401, the Mortgage file must
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain the following documents:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation of the land status and court system that has jurisdiction over the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Indian Loan Guarantee Certificate (HUD Form 53039)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Special Seller warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants with respect to each Section 184 Native American
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The HUD guarantee on each Section 184 Native American Mortgage is in full force and
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect as of the Delivery Date and will not be canceled while Freddie Mac has an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership interest in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Section 184 Native American Mortgage was originated in compliance with all the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Section 184 Regulations, unless those requirements were modified or
elements.label: text

elements.text: waived in writing by HUD and the Bureau of Indian Affairs and Freddie Mac approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: the modifications and waivers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s Indian Tribe received authorization from HUD to participate in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage loan program described in the Section 184 Regulations and adopted, and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforcing, a legal and administrative framework and procedures for foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: evictions, establishing priority of liens and leasing that meets the requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 184 Regulations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The HUD guarantee fee was paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller received the Indian Loan Guarantee Certificate (HUD Form 53039) endorsed

elements.text: for guarantee by an authorized agent of the Assistant Secretary for Public and Indian
elements.label: text

elements.text: Housing

elements.text: (a) General requirements for FHA and VA Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA Mortgages and VA Mortgages are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval before selling FHA and VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages by contacting its Freddie Mac representative or the Contact Support Center at
elements.label: text

elements.text: 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA or VA Mortgages must have the following characteristics:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Mortgage is a fixed-rate, level-payment, fully amortizing, First Lien FHA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: or VA Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The following Mortgages may not be delivered:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Community Land Trust Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Graduated-payment Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Home Improvement Loans (HILs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgages secured by properties subject to income-based resale restrictions that
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminate upon foreclosure (or expiration of any applicable legally required
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure redemption period) or recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, where
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property value must be determined in accordance with Section 4406.4(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Multifamily Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Second Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Super conforming Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Mortgage meets all requirements applicable to the program under which it is
elements.label: text

elements.text: insured or guaranteed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Mortgage meets the requirements set forth in the Guide, except to the extent that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The requirements of the Guide are inconsistent with the requirements of the program
elements.label: text

elements.text: under which the Mortgage is insured or guaranteed, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The requirements of the Guide are specifically modified by this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Mortgage is sold to Freddie Mac with recourse, within the meaning of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: For FHA Mortgages, the Seller must file a Mortgage Record Change (Form HUD-92080)
elements.label: text

elements.text: with HUD to report the sale of an FHA Mortgage and provide HUD with required
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on the Seller or selling mortgagee, the Freddie Mac or purchase or holding
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee and the Servicer. The HUD Mortgagee number for Freddie Mac is 92304-0999-4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Additional requirements for FHA and VA Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding any requirements applicable to the program under which an FHA or VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is insured or guaranteed:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ No Mortgage may have an original maturity in excess of 30 years
elements.label: text
